364: // RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal registers the given signal that will be used to determine if all known
365: // HTTP paths have been registered. It is okay to call this method after instantiating the generic server but before running.
366: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(signalName string, signal <-chan struct{}) error {
367: 	if _, exists := s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName]; exists {
368: 		return fmt.Errorf("%s already registered", signalName)
369: 	}
370: 	s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName] = signal
371: 	return nil
372: }

376: }

378: // RegisterDestroyFunc registers a function that will be called during Destroy().
379: // The function have to be idempotent and prepared to be called more than once.
380: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterDestroyFunc(destroyFn func()) {
381: 	s.destroyFns = append(s.destroyFns, destroyFn)
382: }
383: 
384: // Destroy cleans up all its and its delegation target resources on shutdown.
385: // It starts with destroying its own resources and later proceeds with
386: // its delegation target.
387: func (s *GenericAPIServer) Destroy() {
388: 	for _, destroyFn := range s.destroyFns {
389: 		destroyFn()
390: 	}
391: 	if s.delegationTarget != nil {
392: 		s.delegationTarget.Destroy()
393: 	}
394: }

431: }
432: func (s emptyDelegate) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {
433: 	return map[string]<-chan struct{}{}
434: }
435: func (s emptyDelegate) Destroy() {
436: }
437: 
438: // preparedGenericAPIServer is a private wrapper that enforces a call of PrepareRun() before Run can be invoked.
439: type preparedGenericAPIServer struct {
440: 	*GenericAPIServer
441: }
442: 
443: // PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.
444: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {
445: 	s.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()
446: 
447: 	if s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {
448: 		s.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{
449: 			Config: s.openAPIConfig,
450: 		}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)

17: package rest
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"io"
22: 	"net/http"
23: 	"net/url"
24: 
25: 	"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath"
26: 
27: 	metainternalversion "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion"
28: 	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
29: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
30: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
31: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch"
32: )
33: 
34: //TODO:
35: // Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate
36: // on collections and those that operate on individually named items.
37: // Collection interfaces:
38: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)
39: //    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter
40: //    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher
41: //    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater
42: //    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter
43: //    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater
44: //
45: // Single item interfaces:
46: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)
47: //    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter
48: //    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher
49: //    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater
50: //    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter
51: //    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater
52: 
53: // Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.
54: // Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected
55: // that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.
56: //
57: // Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.
58: type Storage interface {
59: 	// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.
60: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
61: 	New() runtime.Object
62: 
63: 	// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.
64: 	// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared
65: 	// for being called more than once.
66: 	Destroy()
67: }
68: 
69: // StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.
70: type StorageWithReadiness interface {
71: 	Storage
72: 
73: 	// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.
74: 	ReadinessCheck() error
75: }
76: 
77: // Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.
78: // It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.
79: type Scoper interface {
80: 	// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced
81: 	NamespaceScoped() bool
82: }
83: 
84: // KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external
85: // objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for
86: // the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of
87: // API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.
88: type KindProvider interface {
89: 	Kind() string
90: }
91: 
92: // ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.
93: type ShortNamesProvider interface {
94: 	ShortNames() []string
95: }
96: 
97: // CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can
98: // be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. "all" could translate to "pod,rc,svc,...").
99: type CategoriesProvider interface {
100: 	Categories() []string
101: }
102: 
103: // SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular
104: // name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.
105: type SingularNameProvider interface {
106: 	GetSingularName() string
107: }
108: 
109: // GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints
110: // which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.
111: // This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.
112: // TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.
113: type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {
114: 	GroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind
115: }
116: 
117: // GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.
118: // This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).
119: // The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.
120: type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {
121: 	AcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool
122: }
123: 
124: // Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.
125: type Lister interface {
126: 	// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.
127: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
128: 	NewList() runtime.Object
129: 	// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.
130: 	List(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
131: 	// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion
132: 	TableConvertor
133: }
134: 
135: // Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.
136: type Getter interface {
137: 	// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.
138: 	// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the
139: 	// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.
140: 	Get(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
141: }
142: 
143: // GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes
144: // additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the
145: // subpath of the GET request.
146: type GetterWithOptions interface {
147: 	// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.
148: 	// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the
149: 	// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.
150: 	// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions
151: 	// method.
152: 	// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.
153: 	Get(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)
154: 
155: 	// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass
156: 	// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the
157: 	// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named
158: 	// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning "path"
159: 	// will convert the trailing request scheme value to "path" in the map[string][]string
160: 	// passed to the converter.
161: 	NewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)
162: }
163: 
164: type TableConvertor interface {
165: 	ConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)
166: }
167: 
168: // GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a
169: // RESTful object.
170: type GracefulDeleter interface {
171: 	// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.
172: 	// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.
173: 	// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid
174: 	// request error.
175: 	// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the
176: 	// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.
177: 	// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional
178: 	// information about deletion.
179: 	// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly
180: 	// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.
181: 	Delete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)
182: }
183: 
184: // MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.
185: type MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {
186: 	DeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool
187: }
188: 
189: // CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection
190: // of RESTful resources.
191: type CollectionDeleter interface {
192: 	// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'
193: 	// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.
194: 	// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an
195: 	// invalid request error.
196: 	// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still
197: 	// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.
198: 	DeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
199: }
200:

6: You may obtain a copy of the License at
7: 
8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9: 
10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11: distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14: limitations under the License.
15: */
16: 
17: package admission
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"io"
22: 
23: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
24: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
25: 	auditinternal "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit"
26: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
27: )
28: 
29: // Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request
30: // that is used to make an admission decision.
31: type Attributes interface {
32: 	// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client
33: 	// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return
34: 	// the empty string
35: 	GetName() string
36: 	// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)
37: 	GetNamespace() string
38: 	// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods
39: 	GetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource
40: 	// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.
41: 	// For instance, /pods has the resource "pods" and the kind "Pod", while /pods/foo/status has the resource "pods", the sub resource "status", and the kind "Pod"
42: 	// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource "pods", subresource "binding", and kind "Binding".
43: 	GetSubresource() string
44: 	// GetOperation is the operation being performed
45: 	GetOperation() Operation
46: 	// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed
47: 	GetOperationOptions() runtime.Object
48: 	// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent
49: 	// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.
50: 	// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it
51: 	// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.
52: 	IsDryRun() bool
53: 	// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied
54: 	GetObject() runtime.Object
55: 	// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.
56: 	GetOldObject() runtime.Object
57: 	// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod
58: 	GetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind
59: 	// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user
60: 	GetUserInfo() user.Info
61: 
62: 	// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where
63: 	// "podsecuritypolicy" is the name of the plugin, "admission.k8s.io" is the name of the organization, "admit-policy" is the key name.
64: 	// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.
65: 	// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.
66: 	// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or
67: 	// equal to Metadata.
68: 	AddAnnotation(key, value string) error
69: 
70: 	// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.
71: 	// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the
72: 	// intended audit level.
73: 	AddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error
74: 
75: 	// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.
76: 	GetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext
77: }
78: 
79: // ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces
80: // such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope
81: // to handle special cases like CRDs.
82: type ObjectInterfaces interface {
83: 	// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.
84: 	GetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater
85: 	// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.
86: 	GetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper
87: 	// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.
88: 	GetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter
89: 	// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.
90: 	GetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor
91: 	// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.
92: 	GetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper
93: }
94: 
95: // privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.
96: type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {
97: 	getAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string
98: }
99: 
100: // AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement
101: // this interface.
102: type AnnotationsGetter interface {
103: 	GetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string
104: }
105: 
106: // ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.
107: type ReinvocationContext interface {
108: 	// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.
109: 	IsReinvoke() bool
110: 	// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.
111: 	SetIsReinvoke()
112: 	// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.
113: 	ShouldReinvoke() bool
114: 	// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.
115: 	SetShouldReinvoke()
116: 	// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.
117: 	SetValue(plugin string, v interface{})
118: 	// Value reads a value for a webhook.
119: 	Value(plugin string) interface{}
120: }
121: 
122: // Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.
123: type Interface interface {
124: 	// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation
125: 	// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT
126: 	Handles(operation Operation) bool
127: }
128: 
129: type MutationInterface interface {
130: 	Interface
131: 
132: 	// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.
133: 	// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.
134: 	Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)
135: }
136: 
137: // ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.
138: type ValidationInterface interface {
139: 	Interface
140: 
141: 	// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate
142: 	// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.
143: 	Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)
144: }
145: 
146: // Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control
147: type Operation string
148: 
149: // Operation constants
150: const (
151: 	Create  Operation = "CREATE"
152: 	Update  Operation = "UPDATE"
153: 	Delete  Operation = "DELETE"
154: 	Connect Operation = "CONNECT"
155: )
156: 
157: // PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.
158: // After initialization the resources have to be set separately
159: type PluginInitializer interface {
160: 	Initialize(plugin Interface)
161: }
162: 
163: // InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized
164: // shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins
165: type InitializationValidator interface {
166: 	ValidateInitialization() error
167: }
168: 
169: // ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name
170: type ConfigProvider interface {
171: 	ConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)
172: }
173: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go"}

{"content":"200: 
201: // Creater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object.
202: type Creater interface {
203: 	// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.
204: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
205: 	New() runtime.Object
206: 
207: 	// Create creates a new version of a resource.
208: 	Create(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
209: }
210: 
211: // NamedCreater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object using a name parameter.
212: type NamedCreater interface {
213: 	// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.
214: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
215: 	New() runtime.Object
216: 
217: 	// Create creates a new version of a resource. It expects a name parameter from the path.
218: 	// This is needed for create operations on subresources which include the name of the parent
219: 	// resource in the path.
220: 	Create(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)
221: }
222: 
223: // SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver adds configuration options to a Creater for subresources.
224: type SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver interface {
225: 	// PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate indicates that a
226: 	// handler should preserve fields of ObjectMeta that are managed by the system.
227: 	PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate() bool
228: }
229: 
230: // UpdatedObjectInfo provides information about an updated object to an Updater.
231: // It requires access to the old object in order to return the newly updated object.
232: type UpdatedObjectInfo interface {
233: 	// Returns preconditions built from the updated object, if applicable.
234: 	// May return nil, or a preconditions object containing nil fields,
235: 	// if no preconditions can be determined from the updated object.
236: 	Preconditions() *metav1.Preconditions
237: 
238: 	// UpdatedObject returns the updated object, given a context and old object.
239: 	// The only time an empty oldObj should be passed in is if a "create on update" is occurring (there is no oldObj).
240: 	UpdatedObject(ctx context.Context, oldObj runtime.Object) (newObj runtime.Object, err error)
241: }
242: 
243: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned
244: // if the hook cannot be completed. A ValidateObjectFunc may NOT transform the provided
245: // object.
246: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error
247: 
248: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a "admit everything" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.
249: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {
250: 	return nil
251: }
252: 
253: // ValidateObjectUpdateFunc is a function to act on a given object and its predecessor.
254: // An error may be returned if the hook cannot be completed. An UpdateObjectFunc
255: // may NOT transform the provided object.
256: type ValidateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error
257: 
258: // ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc is a "admit everything" instance of ValidateObjectUpdateFunc.
259: func ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error {
260: 	return nil
261: }
262: 
263: // Updater is an object that can update an instance of a RESTful object.
264: type Updater interface {
265: 	// New returns an empty object that can be used with Update after request data has been put into it.
266: 	// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)
267: 	New() runtime.Object
268: 
269: 	// Update finds a resource in the storage and updates it. Some implementations
270: 	// may allow updates creates the object - they should set the created boolean
271: 	// to true.
272: 	Update(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)
273: }
274: 
275: // CreaterUpdater is a storage object that must support both create and update.
276: // Go prevents embedded interfaces that implement the same method.
277: type CreaterUpdater interface {
278: 	Creater
279: 	Update(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)
280: }
281: 
282: // CreaterUpdater must satisfy the Updater interface.
283: var _ Updater = CreaterUpdater(nil)
284: 
285: // Patcher is a storage object that supports both get and update.
286: type Patcher interface {
287: 	Getter
288: 	Updater
289: }
290: 
291: // Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that
292: // want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.
293: type Watcher interface {
294: 	// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields
295: 	// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that
296: 	// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a
297: 	// particular version.
298: 	Watch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)
299: }
300: 
301: // StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a
302: // resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.
303: type StandardStorage interface {
304: 	Getter
305: 	Lister
306: 	CreaterUpdater
307: 	GracefulDeleter
308: 	CollectionDeleter
309: 	Watcher
310: 
311: 	// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.
312: 	// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared
313: 	// for being called more than once.
314: 	Destroy()
315: }
316: 
317: // Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.
318: type Redirector interface {
319: 	// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.
320: 	ResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)
321: }
322: 
323: // Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that
324: // may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object
325: // behavior in other cases.
326: type Responder interface {
327: 	// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.
328: 	Object(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)
329: 	// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.
330: 	Error(err error)
331: }
332: 
333: // Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.
334: type Connecter interface {
335: 	// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.
336: 	// The provided responder may be used for common API responses. The responder will write both status
337: 	// code and body, so the ServeHTTP method should exit after invoking the responder. The Handler will
338: 	// be used for a single API request and then discarded. The Responder is guaranteed to write to the
339: 	// same http.ResponseWriter passed to ServeHTTP.
340: 	Connect(ctx context.Context, id string, options runtime.Object, r Responder) (http.Handler, error)
341: 
342: 	// NewConnectOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass
343: 	// options to the Connect method. If nil, then a nil options object is passed to
344: 	// Connect. It may return a bool and a string. If true, the value of the request
345: 	// path below the object will be included as the named string in the serialization
346: 	// of the runtime object.
347: 	NewConnectOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)
348: 
349: 	// ConnectMethods returns the list of HTTP methods handled by Connect
350: 	ConnectMethods() []string
351: }
352: 
353: // ResourceStreamer is an interface implemented by objects that prefer to be streamed from the server
354: // instead of decoded directly.
355: type ResourceStreamer interface {
356: 	// InputStream should return an io.ReadCloser if the provided object supports streaming. The desired
357: 	// api version and an accept header (may be empty) are passed to the call. If no error occurs,
358: 	// the caller may return a flag indicating whether the result should be flushed as writes occur
359: 	// and a content type string that indicates the type of the stream.
360: 	// If a null stream is returned, a StatusNoContent response wil be generated.
361: 	InputStream(ctx context.Context, apiVersion, acceptHeader string) (stream io.ReadCloser, flush bool, mimeType string, err error)
362: }
363: 
364: // StorageMetadata is an optional interface that callers can implement to provide additional
365: // information about their Storage objects.
366: type StorageMetadata interface {
367: 	// ProducesMIMETypes returns a list of the MIME types the specified HTTP verb (GET, POST, DELETE,
368: 	// PATCH) can respond with.
369: 	ProducesMIMETypes(verb string) []string
370: 
371: 	// ProducesObject returns an object the specified HTTP verb respond with. It will overwrite storage object if
372: 	// it is not nil. Only the type of the return object matters, the value will be ignored.
373: 	ProducesObject(verb string) interface{}
374: }
375: 
376: // StorageVersionProvider is an optional interface that a storage object can
377: // implement if it wishes to disclose its storage version.
378: type StorageVersionProvider interface {
379: 	// StorageVersion returns a group versioner, which will outputs the gvk
380: 	// an object will be converted to before persisted in etcd, given a
381: 	// list of kinds the object might belong to.
382: 	StorageVersion() runtime.GroupVersioner
383: }
384: 
385: // ResetFieldsStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can
386: // implement if it wishes to provide the fields reset by its strategies.
387: type ResetFieldsStrategy interface {
388: 	GetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set
389: }
390: 
391: // ResetFieldsFilterStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can
392: // implement if it wishes to provide a fields filter reset by its strategies.
393: type ResetFieldsFilterStrategy interface {
394: 	GetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter
395: }
396: 
397: // CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy is a union of RESTCreateUpdateStrategy
398: // and ResetFieldsStrategy.
399: type CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy interface {
400: 	RESTCreateUpdateStrategy

17: package storage
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"fmt"
22: 	"strings"
23: 
24: 	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
25: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta"
26: 	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"

30: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"

34: // Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.
35: type Feature = string
36: 
37: // RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.
38: var RequestWatchProgress Feature = "RequestWatchProgress"
39: 
40: // Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response
41: // onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an
42: // object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be
43: // a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,
44: // intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of
45: // type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.
46: type Versioner interface {
47: 	// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object
48: 	// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata
49: 	// from database.
50: 	UpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error
51: 	// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object
52: 	// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from
53: 	// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client
54: 	// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number
55: 	// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during
56: 	// serialization if it is set to nil.
57: 	UpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error
58: 	// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should
59: 	// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.
60: 	PrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error
61: 	// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.
62: 	// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.
63: 	ObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)
64: 
65: 	// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and
66: 	// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().
67: 	// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch
68: 	// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass
69: 	// "1", you will see updates from "2" onwards).
70: 	ParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)
71: }
72: 
73: // ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with
74: // an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete
75: // database and to improve testability.
76: type ResponseMeta struct {
77: 	// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be
78: 	// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested
79: 	// expiration time due to server lag).
80: 	TTL int64
81: 	// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.
82: 	ResourceVersion uint64
83: }
84: 
85: // IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes
86: // `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.
87: type IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string
88: 
89: // IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that
90: // for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.
91: type IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc
92: 
93: // Everything accepts all objects.
94: var Everything = SelectionPredicate{
95: 	Label: labels.Everything(),
96: 	Field: fields.Everything(),
97: }
98: 
99: // MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).
100: type MatchValue struct {
101: 	IndexName string
102: 	Value     string
103: }
104: 
105: // Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update
106: // that is guaranteed to succeed.
107: // See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.
108: type UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)
109: 
110: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned
111: // if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided
112: // object.
113: // NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the
114: // storage, due to transformation or decode error.
115: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error
116: 
117: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a "admit everything" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.
118: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {
119: 	return nil
120: }
121: 
122: // Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.
123: type Preconditions struct {
124: 	// Specifies the target UID.
125: 	// +optional
126: 	UID *types.UID `json:"uid,omitempty"`
127: 	// Specifies the target ResourceVersion
128: 	// +optional
129: 	ResourceVersion *string `json:"resourceVersion,omitempty"`
130: }
131: 
132: // NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.
133: func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {
134: 	u := types.UID(uid)
135: 	return &Preconditions{UID: &u}
136: }
137: 
138: func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {
139: 	if p == nil {
140: 		return nil
141: 	}
142: 	objMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return NewInternalError(
145: 			fmt.Errorf("can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w",
146: 				*p,
147: 				obj,
148: 				err))
149: 	}
150: 	if p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {
151: 		err := fmt.Sprintf(
152: 			"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v",
153: 			*p.UID,
154: 			objMeta.GetUID())
155: 		return NewInvalidObjError(key, err)
156: 	}
157: 	if p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {
158: 		err := fmt.Sprintf(
159: 			"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v",
160: 			*p.ResourceVersion,
161: 			objMeta.GetResourceVersion())
162: 		return NewInvalidObjError(key, err)
163: 	}
164: 	return nil
165: }

167: // Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and
168: // hides all the storage-related operations behind it.
169: type Interface interface {
170: 	// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.
171: 	Versioner() Versioner
172: 
173: 	// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live
174: 	// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be
175: 	// set to the read value from database.
176: 	Create(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error
177: 
178: 	// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.
179: 	// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.
180: 	// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the
181: 	// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.
182: 	// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.
183: 	Delete(
184: 		ctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,
185: 		validateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error
186: 
187: 	// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,
188: 	// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.
189: 	// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,
190: 	// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1
191: 	// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).
192: 	// If resource version is "0", this interface will get current object at given key
193: 	// and send it in an "ADDED" event, before watch starts.
194: 	Watch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)
195: 
196: 	// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either
197: 	// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.
198: 	// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.
199: 	// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will
200: 	// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.

{"content":"200: 	// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.
201: 	Get(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error
202: 
203: 	// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object
204: 	// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).
205: 	// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'
206: 	// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.
207: 	// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will
208: 	// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.
209: 	GetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error
210: 
211: 	// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')
212: 	// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.
213: 	// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if
214: 	// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account
215: 	// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.
216: 	// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false
217: 	// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.
218: 	// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized
219: 	// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those
220: 	// contents.
221: 	// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the
222: 	// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.
223: 	// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.
224: 	//
225: 	// Example:
226: 	//
227: 	// s := /* implementation of Interface */
228: 	// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(
229: 	//     "myKey", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,
230: 	//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {
231: 	//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, "input" is reset to
232: 	//       // current contents for "myKey" in database.
233: 	//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.
234: 	//
235: 	//       // Make the modification
236: 	//       curr.Counter++
237: 	//
238: 	//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return
239: 	//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.
240: 	//       return cur, nil, nil
241: 	//    }, cachedExistingObject
242: 	// )
243: 	GuaranteedUpdate(
244: 		ctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,
245: 		preconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error
246: 
247: 	// Stats returns storage stats.
248: 	Stats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)
249: 
250: 	// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.
251: 	ReadinessCheck() error
252: 
253: 	// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the
254: 	// watch response stream as soon as possible.
255: 	// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.
256: 	//
257: 	// If watch is lagging, progress status might:
258: 	// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.
259: 	// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.
260: 	//
261: 	// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.
262: 	// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042
263: 	//
264: 	// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.
265: 	// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.
266: 	RequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error
267: 
268: 	// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.
269: 	// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata
270: 	GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)
271: 
272: 	// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.
273: 	EnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error
274: 
275: 	// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.
276: 	// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.
277: 	// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.
278: 	CompactRevision() int64
279: }
280: 
281: // KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.
282: type KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)
283: 
284: // GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.
285: type GetOptions struct {
286: 	// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If
287: 	// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.
288: 	IgnoreNotFound bool
289: 	// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation
290: 	// as a "not older than" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided
291: 	// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this
292: 	// ResourceVersion may be served.
293: 	ResourceVersion string
294: }
295: 
296: // ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.
297: type ListOptions struct {
298: 	// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation
299: 	// as a "not older than" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided
300: 	// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this
301: 	// ResourceVersion may be served.
302: 	ResourceVersion string
303: 	// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set
304: 	// to the default value "" the legacy resource version semantic apply.
305: 	ResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch
306: 	// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.
307: 	Predicate SelectionPredicate
308: 	// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the
309: 	// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.
310: 	Recursive bool
311: 	// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should
312: 	// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.
313: 	//
314: 	// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.
315: 	// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.
316: 	//
317: 	// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.
318: 	// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.
319: 	//
320: 	// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency
321: 	// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.
322: 	// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.
323: 	//
324: 	// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,
325: 	// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal
326: 	ProgressNotify bool
327: 	// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,
328: 	// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the
329: 	// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic "Bookmark"
330: 	// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server

38: // ConvertabilityChecker indicates what versions a GroupKind is available in.
39: type ConvertabilityChecker interface {
40: 	// VersionsForGroupKind indicates what versions are available to convert a group kind. This determines
41: 	// what our decoding abilities are.
42: 	VersionsForGroupKind(gk schema.GroupKind) []schema.GroupVersion
43: }
44: 
45: // APIGroupVersion is a helper for exposing rest.Storage objects as http.Handlers via go-restful
46: // It handles URLs of the form:
47: // /${storage_key}[/${object_name}]
48: // Where 'storage_key' points to a rest.Storage object stored in storage.
49: // This object should contain all parameterization necessary for running a particular API version
50: type APIGroupVersion struct {
51: 	Storage map[string]rest.Storage
52: 
53: 	Root string
54: 
55: 	// GroupVersion is the external group version
56: 	GroupVersion schema.GroupVersion
57: 
58: 	// AllServedVersionsByResource is indexed by resource and maps to a list of versions that resource exists in.
59: 	// This was created so that StorageVersion for APIs can include a list of all version that are served for each
60: 	// GroupResource tuple.
61: 	AllServedVersionsByResource map[string][]string
62: 
63: 	// OptionsExternalVersion controls the Kubernetes APIVersion used for common objects in the apiserver
64: 	// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may
65: 	// define a version "v1beta1" but want to use the Kubernetes "v1" internal objects. If
66: 	// empty, defaults to GroupVersion.
67: 	OptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion
68: 	// MetaGroupVersion defaults to "meta.k8s.io/v1" and is the scheme group version used to decode
69: 	// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group
70: 	// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).
71: 	MetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion
72: 
73: 	// Serializer is used to determine how to convert responses from API methods into bytes to send over
74: 	// the wire.
75: 	Serializer     runtime.NegotiatedSerializer
76: 	ParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec
77: 
78: 	Typer                 runtime.ObjectTyper
79: 	Creater               runtime.ObjectCreater
80: 	Convertor             runtime.ObjectConvertor
81: 	ConvertabilityChecker ConvertabilityChecker
82: 	Defaulter             runtime.ObjectDefaulter
83: 	Namer                 runtime.Namer
84: 	UnsafeConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor
85: 	TypeConverter         managedfields.TypeConverter
86: 
87: 	EquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry
88: 
89: 	// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary
90: 	// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in
91: 	// the create-on-update case
92: 	Authorizer authorizer.Authorizer
93: 
94: 	Admit admission.Interface
95: 
96: 	MinRequestTimeout time.Duration
97: 
98: 	// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.
99: 	// 0 means no limit.
100: 	MaxRequestBodyBytes int64
101: }
102: 
103: // InstallREST registers the REST handlers (storage, watch, proxy and redirect) into a restful Container.
104: // It is expected that the provided path root prefix will serve all operations. Root MUST NOT end
105: // in a slash.
106: func (g *APIGroupVersion) InstallREST(container *restful.Container) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {
107: 	prefix := path.Join(g.Root, g.GroupVersion.Group, g.GroupVersion.Version)
108: 	installer := &APIInstaller{
109: 		group:             g,
110: 		prefix:            prefix,
111: 		minRequestTimeout: g.MinRequestTimeout,
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	apiResources, resourceInfos, ws, registrationErrors := installer.Install()
115: 	versionDiscoveryHandler := discovery.NewAPIVersionHandler(g.Serializer, g.GroupVersion, staticLister{apiResources})
116: 	versionDiscoveryHandler.AddToWebService(ws)
117: 	container.Add(ws)
118: 	aggregatedDiscoveryResources, err := ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(apiResources)
119: 	if err != nil {
120: 		registrationErrors = append(registrationErrors, err)

62: type APIInstaller struct {
63: 	group             *APIGroupVersion
64: 	prefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.
65: 	minRequestTimeout time.Duration
66: }
67: 
68: // Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).
69: type action struct {
70: 	Verb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).
71: 	Path          string               // The path of the action
72: 	Params        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.
73: 	Namer         handlers.ScopeNamer
74: 	AllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces
75: }
76: 
77: func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {
78: 	var apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery
79: 	parentResources := make(map[string]int)
80: 
81: 	// Loop through all top-level resources
82: 	for _, r := range list {
83: 		if strings.Contains(r.Name, "/") {
84: 			// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources
85: 			continue
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		var scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope
89: 		if r.Namespaced {
90: 			scope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace
91: 		} else {
92: 			scope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster
93: 		}
94: 
95: 		resource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{
96: 			Resource: r.Name,
97: 			Scope:    scope,
98: 			ResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{
99: 				Group:   r.Group,
100: 				Version: r.Version,
101: 				Kind:    r.Kind,
102: 			},
103: 			Verbs:            r.Verbs,
104: 			ShortNames:       r.ShortNames,
105: 			Categories:       r.Categories,
106: 			SingularResource: r.SingularName,
107: 		}
108: 		apiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)
109: 		parentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	// Loop through all subresources
113: 	for _, r := range list {
114: 		// Split resource name and subresource name
115: 		split := strings.SplitN(r.Name, "/", 2)
116: 
117: 		if len(split) != 2 {
118: 			// Skip parent resources
119: 			continue
120: 		}

12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14: limitations under the License.

16: 
17: package filters
18: 
19: import (
20: 	"context"
21: 	"errors"
22: 	"fmt"
23: 	"net/http"
24: 	"time"
25: 
26: 	apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
27: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
28: 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
29: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator"
30: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory"
31: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest"
32: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user"
33: 	"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters"
34: 	genericapirequest "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request"
35: 	genericfeatures "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features"
36: 	utilfeature "k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature"
37: 	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
38: )
39: 
40: type authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)
41: 
42: // WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then
43: // stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error
44: // the failed handler is used. On success, "Authorization" header is removed from the request and handler
45: // is invoked to serve the request.
46: func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {
47: 	return withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)
48: }
49: 
50: func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {
51: 	if auth == nil {
52: 		klog.Warning("Authentication is disabled")
53: 		return handler
54: 	}
55: 	standardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{
56: 		UsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-User"},
57: 		UIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Uid"},
58: 		GroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Group"},
59: 		ExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{"X-Remote-Extra-"},
60: 	}
61: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
62: 		authenticationStart := time.Now()
63: 
64: 		if len(apiAuds) > 0 {
65: 			req = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))
66: 		}
67: 		resp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)
68: 		authenticationFinish := time.Now()
69: 		genericapirequest.TrackAuthenticationLatency(req.Context(), authenticationFinish.Sub(authenticationStart))
70: 		defer func() {
71: 			metrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)
72: 		}()
73: 		if err != nil || !ok {
74: 			if err != nil {
75: 				klog.ErrorS(err, "Unable to authenticate the request")
76: 			}
77: 			failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
78: 			return
79: 		}
80: 
81: 		if !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {
82: 			err = fmt.Errorf("unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)
83: 			klog.Error(err)
84: 			failed.ServeHTTP(w, req)
85: 			return
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.
89: 		req.Header.Del("Authorization")
90: 
91: 		// delete standard front proxy headers
92: 		headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
93: 			req.Header,
94: 			standardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
95: 			standardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
96: 			standardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
97: 			standardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
98: 		)
99: 
100: 		// also delete any custom front proxy headers
101: 		if requestHeaderConfig != nil {
102: 			headerrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(
103: 				req.Header,
104: 				requestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,
105: 				requestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,
106: 				requestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,
107: 				requestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,
108: 			)
109: 		}
110: 
111: 		// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
112: 		// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
113: 		// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
114: 		// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
115: 		// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).
116: 		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {
117: 			// limit this connection to just this request,
118: 			// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
119: 			// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
120: 			w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
121: 		}
122: 		req = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))
123: 		handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
124: 	})
125: }
126: 
127: func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
128: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
129: 		// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,
130: 		// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.
131: 		// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these
132: 		// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the
133: 		// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).
134: 		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {
135: 			// limit this connection to just this request,
136: 			// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection
137: 			// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c
138: 			w.Header().Set("Connection", "close")
139: 		}
140: 		ctx := req.Context()
141: 		requestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)
142: 		if !found {
143: 			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New("no RequestInfo found in the context"))
144: 			return
145: 		}
146: 
147: 		gv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}
148: 		responsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized("Unauthorized"), s, gv, w, req)
149: 	})
150: }
151: 
152: func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {
153: 	if len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {
154: 		return true
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	return len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0
158: }
159: 
160: func isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {
161: 	if u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {
162: 		return true
163: 	}
164: 	for _, group := range u.GetGroups() {
165: 		if group == user.AllUnauthenticated {
166: 			return true
167: 		}
168: 	}
169: 	return false
170: }

39: const (
40: 	// Annotation key names set in advanced audit
41: 	decisionAnnotationKey = "authorization.k8s.io/decision"
42: 	reasonAnnotationKey   = "authorization.k8s.io/reason"
43: 
44: 	// Annotation values set in advanced audit
45: 	decisionAllow  = "allow"
46: 	decisionForbid = "forbid"
47: 	reasonError    = "internal error"
48: )
49: 
50: type recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)
51: 
52: // WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.
53: func WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {
54: 	return withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)
55: }
56: 
57: func withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {
58: 	if a == nil {
59: 		klog.Warning("Authorization is disabled")
60: 		return handler
61: 	}
62: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
63: 		ctx := req.Context()
64: 		authorizationStart := time.Now()
65: 
66: 		attributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)
67: 		if err != nil {
68: 			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)
69: 			return
70: 		}
71: 		authorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)
72: 
73: 		authorizationFinish := time.Now()
74: 		request.TrackAuthorizationLatency(ctx, authorizationFinish.Sub(authorizationStart))
75: 		defer func() {
76: 			metrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)
77: 		}()
78: 
79: 		// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.
80: 		if authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {
81: 			audit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,
82: 				decisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,
83: 				reasonAnnotationKey, reason)
84: 			handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)
85: 			return
86: 		}
87: 		if err != nil {
88: 			audit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)
89: 			responsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)
90: 			return
91: 		}
92: 
93: 		klog.V(4).InfoS("Forbidden", "URI", req.RequestURI, "reason", reason)
94: 		audit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,
95: 			decisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,
96: 			reasonAnnotationKey, reason)
97: 		responsewriters.Forbidden(attributes, w, req, reason, s)
98: 	})
99: }
100: 
101: func GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {
102: 	attribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}
103: 
104: 	user, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)
105: 	if ok {
106: 		attribs.User = user
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	requestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)
110: 	if !found {
111: 		return nil, errors.New("no RequestInfo found in the context")
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests
115: 	attribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest
116: 	attribs.Path = requestInfo.Path
117: 	attribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb
118: 
119: 	attribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup
120: 	attribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion
121: 	attribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource
122: 	attribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource
123: 	attribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace
124: 	attribs.Name = requestInfo.Name
125: 
126: 	if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {
127: 		// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when
128: 		// doing shallow copies in other steps.
129: 		if len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {
130: 			fieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)
131: 			if err != nil {
132: 				attribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err
133: 			} else {
134: 				if requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {
135: 					attribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil
136: 				}
137: 			}
138: 		}
139: 
140: 		if len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {
141: 			labelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)
142: 			if err != nil {
143: 				attribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err
144: 			} else {
145: 				if requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {
146: 					attribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil
147: 				}
148: 			}
149: 		}
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	return &attribs, nil
153: }

1022: func BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {
1023: 	// WithStorageVersionPrecondition needs the WithRequestInfo to run first
1024: 	handler := genericapifilters.WithStorageVersionPrecondition(apiHandler, c.StorageVersionManager, c.Serializer)
1025: 	return DefaultBuildHandlerChain(handler, c)
1026: }
1027: 
1028: func DefaultBuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {
1029: 	handler := apiHandler
1030: 
1031: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)
1032: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithAuthorization(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)
1033: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "authorization")
1034: 
1035: 	if c.FlowControl != nil {
1036: 		workEstimatorCfg := flowcontrolrequest.DefaultWorkEstimatorConfig()
1037: 		requestWorkEstimator := flowcontrolrequest.NewWorkEstimator(
1038: 			c.StorageObjectCountTracker.Get, c.FlowControl.GetInterestedWatchCount, workEstimatorCfg, c.FlowControl.GetMaxSeats)
1039: 		handler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)
1040: 		handler = genericfilters.WithPriorityAndFairness(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.FlowControl, requestWorkEstimator, c.RequestTimeout/4)
1041: 		handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "priorityandfairness")
1042: 	} else {
1043: 		handler = genericfilters.WithMaxInFlightLimit(handler, c.MaxRequestsInFlight, c.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight, c.LongRunningFunc)
1044: 	}
1045: 
1046: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)
1047: 	if c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.ConstrainedImpersonation) {
1048: 		handler = impersonation.WithConstrainedImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)
1049: 		handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "constrainedimpersonation")
1050: 	} else {
1051: 		handler = impersonation.WithImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)
1052: 		handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "impersonation")
1053: 	}
1054: 
1055: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)
1056: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithAudit(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator, c.LongRunningFunc)
1057: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "audit")
1058: 
1059: 	failedHandler := genericapifilters.Unauthorized(c.Serializer)
1060: 	failedHandler = genericapifilters.WithFailedAuthenticationAudit(failedHandler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator)
1061: 
1062: 	// WithTracing comes after authentication so we can allow authenticated
1063: 	// clients to influence sampling.
1064: 	if c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {
1065: 		handler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)
1066: 	}
1067: 	failedHandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(failedHandler)
1068: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)
1069: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, c.Authentication.Authenticator, failedHandler, c.Authentication.APIAudiences, c.Authentication.RequestHeaderConfig)
1070: 	handler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, "authentication")
1071: 
1072: 	handler = genericfilters.WithCORS(handler, c.CorsAllowedOriginList, nil, nil, nil, "true")
1073: 
1074: 	// WithWarningRecorder must be wrapped by the timeout handler
1075: 	// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe
1076: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)
1077: 
1078: 	// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the
1079: 	// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.
1080: 	handler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)
1081: 
1082: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,
1083: 		c.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)
1084: 	handler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)
1085: 	if c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {
1086: 		handler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)
1087: 	}
1088: 	if c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {
1089: 		handler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)
1090: 	}
1091: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)
1092: 	handler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)
1093: 	if c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {
1094: 		handler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())
1095: 	}
1096: 	handler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)
1097: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)
1098: 	// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving
1099: 	// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be
1100: 	// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.
1101: 	if c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {
1102: 		handler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)
1103: 	}
1104: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)
1105: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)
1106: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())
1107: 	handler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)
1108: 	handler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)
1109: 	return handler
1110: }

1112: func installAPI(name string, s *GenericAPIServer, c *Config) {
1113: 	if c.EnableIndex {
1114: 		routes.Index{}.Install(s.listedPathProvider, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)
1115: 	}
1116: 	if c.EnableProfiling {
1117: 		routes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)
1118: 		if c.EnableContentionProfiling {
1119: 			goruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)
1120: 		}

84: // Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's
85: // intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any
86: // non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be
87: // copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same
88: // underlying behavior.
89: //
90: // All fields are required unless specified.
91: //
92: // The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific
93: // RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike
94: // resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and
95: // semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and
96: // RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic
97: // specific to the API.
98: //
99: // TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage
100: type Store struct {
101: 	// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a
102: 	// GET of a single object, e.g.:
103: 	//
104: 	// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object
105: 	NewFunc func() runtime.Object
106: 
107: 	// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the
108: 	// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:
109: 	//
110: 	// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource
111: 	NewListFunc func() runtime.Object
112: 
113: 	// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.
114: 	// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.
115: 	// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.
116: 	DefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource
117: 
118: 	// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.
119: 	SingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource

463: 	readinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()
464: 	err := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)
465: 	if err != nil {
466: 		klog.Errorf("Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s", err)
467: 	}
468: 	s.installReadyz()

470: 	componentName := "apiserver"
471: 	if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {
472: 		statusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))
473: 	}
474: 
475: 	if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {
476: 		if s.Flagz != nil {
477: 			flagz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, s.Flagz)
478: 		}
479: 	}

481: 	return preparedGenericAPIServer{s}

484: // Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed
485: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.
486: //
487: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid
488: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.
489: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {
490: 	ctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)
491: 	return s.RunWithContext(ctx)
492: }
