494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled
495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.
496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:
497: //
498: // |                                   ctx
499: // |                                    |
500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------
501: // |           |                                                       |
502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |
503: // |           |                                                       |
504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)
505: // |           |                                                       |
506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)
507: // |           |                                                       |
508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|
509: // |                                    |
510: // |                                    |
511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)
512: // |                                    |
513: // |                                    |
514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |
516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |
517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |
518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |
519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |
520: // |           |                                       |                                          |
521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |
522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |
523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |
524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |
525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |
526: // |           |                                         |                                        |
527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |
528: // |           |                                         |                                        |
529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|
530: // |                               |                     |
531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())
532: // |                               |
533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh
534: // |                               |
535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)
536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {
537: 	stopCh := ctx.Done()
538: 	delayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration
539: 	shutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated
540: 
541: 	// Clean up resources on shutdown.
542: 	defer s.Destroy()
543: 
544: 	// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket
545: 	if s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {
546: 		go func() {
547: 			defer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)

549: 		}()
550: 	}
551: 
552: 	// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal
553: 	// registration happens during construction of the generic api server
554: 	// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances
555: 	go func() {
556: 		for _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {
557: 			select {
558: 			case <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:
559: 				continue
560: 			case <-stopCh:
561: 				klog.V(1).Infof("haven't completed %s, stop requested", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())
562: 				return
563: 			}
564: 		}
565: 		s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()

567: 	}()

569: 	go func() {
570: 		defer delayedStopCh.Signal()
571: 		defer klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", delayedStopCh.Name())
572: 
573: 		<-stopCh
574: 
575: 		// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.
576: 		// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red
577: 		// and stop sending traffic to this server.

581: 		time.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)

583: 
584: 	// close socket after delayed stopCh
585: 	shutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout
586: 	if s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {

588: 		// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight
589: 		//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.
590: 		// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,

592: 		//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s

602: 	go func() {
603: 		defer stopHTTPServer(errors.New("time to stop HTTP server"))
604: 
605: 		timeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()
606: 		if s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {
607: 			timeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()
608: 		}
609: 
610: 		<-timeToStopHttpServerCh
611: 	}()

613: 	// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run
614: 	// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.
615: 	// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.
616: 	if s.AuditBackend != nil {
617: 		if err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {
618: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to run the audit backend: %v", err)

620: 	}
621: 
622: 	stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)
623: 	if err != nil {
624: 		return err
625: 	}
626: 
627: 	httpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening

629: 		<-listenerStoppedCh
630: 		httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()

632: 	}()

634: 	// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has

636: 	preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped

638: 		defer klog.V(1).InfoS("[graceful-termination] shutdown event", "name", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())
639: 		defer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()
640: 
641: 		// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain
642: 		<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()
643: 
644: 		// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need

656: 		// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).

659: 		// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.

680: 		<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()

748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is
749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.
750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.
751: //

759: // NonBlockingRunWithContext spawns the secure http server. An error is
760: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.
761: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.
762: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx context.Context, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {
763: 	// Use an internal stop channel to allow cleanup of the listeners on error.
764: 	internalStopCh := make(chan struct{})
765: 	var stoppedCh <-chan struct{}
766: 	var listenerStoppedCh <-chan struct{}
767: 	if s.SecureServingInfo != nil && s.Handler != nil {
768: 		var err error
769: 		stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err = s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, internalStopCh)
770: 		if err != nil {
771: 			close(internalStopCh)
772: 			return nil, nil, err
773: 		}
774: 	}
775: 
776: 	// Now that listener have bound successfully, it is the
777: 	// responsibility of the caller to close the provided channel to
778: 	// ensure cleanup.
779: 	go func() {
780: 		<-ctx.Done()
781: 		close(internalStopCh)
782: 	}()
783: 
784: 	s.RunPostStartHooks(ctx)
785: 
786: 	if _, err := systemd.SdNotify(true, "READY=1\n"); err != nil {
787: 		klog.Errorf("Unable to send systemd daemon successful start message: %v\n", err)
788: 	}
789: 
790: 	return stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, nil
791: }

794: func (s *GenericAPIServer) installAPIResources(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, typeConverter managedfields.TypeConverter) error {
795: 	var resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo
796: 	for _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {
797: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {
798: 			klog.Warningf("Skipping API %v because it has no resources.", groupVersion)

801: 
802: 		apiGroupVersion, err := s.getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion, apiPrefix)
803: 		if err != nil {
804: 			return err
805: 		}
806: 		if apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {
807: 			apiGroupVersion.OptionsExternalVersion = apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion
808: 		}
809: 		apiGroupVersion.TypeConverter = typeConverter
810: 		apiGroupVersion.MaxRequestBodyBytes = s.maxRequestBodyBytes
811: 
812: 		discoveryAPIResources, r, err := apiGroupVersion.InstallREST(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)
813: 
814: 		if err != nil {
815: 			return fmt.Errorf("unable to setup API %v: %v", apiGroupInfo, err)
816: 		}
817: 		resourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, r...)
818: 
819: 		// Aggregated discovery only aggregates resources under /apis
820: 		if apiPrefix == APIGroupPrefix {
821: 			s.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(
822: 				groupVersion.Group,
823: 				apidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{
824: 					Freshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,
825: 					Version:   groupVersion.Version,
826: 					Resources: discoveryAPIResources,
827: 				},
828: 			)
829: 		} else {
830: 			// There is only one group version for legacy resources, priority can be defaulted to 0.
831: 			s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(
832: 				groupVersion.Group,
833: 				apidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{
834: 					Freshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,
835: 					Version:   groupVersion.Version,
836: 					Resources: discoveryAPIResources,
837: 				},
838: 			)
839: 		}
840: 
841: 	}
842: 
843: 	s.RegisterDestroyFunc(apiGroupInfo.destroyStorage)
844: 
845: 	if s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionAPI) &&
846: 		s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.APIServerIdentity) {
847: 		// API installation happens before we start listening on the handlers,
848: 		// therefore it is safe to register ResourceInfos here. The handler will block
849: 		// write requests until the storage versions of the targeting resources are updated.
850: 		s.StorageVersionManager.AddResourceInfo(resourceInfos...)
851: 	}
852: 
853: 	return nil
854: }
855: 
856: // InstallLegacyAPIGroup exposes the given legacy api group in the API.
857: // The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the
858: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.
859: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallLegacyAPIGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {
860: 	if !s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.Has(apiPrefix) {
861: 		return fmt.Errorf("%q is not in the allowed legacy API prefixes: %v", apiPrefix, s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.List())
862: 	}
863: 
864: 	openAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo)
865: 	if err != nil {
866: 		return fmt.Errorf("unable to get openapi models: %v", err)
867: 	}
868: 
869: 	if err := s.installAPIResources(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {
870: 		return err
871: 	}
872: 
873: 	// Install the version handler.
874: 	// Add a handler at /<apiPrefix> to enumerate the supported api versions.
875: 	legacyRootAPIHandler := discovery.NewLegacyRootAPIHandler(s.discoveryAddresses, s.Serializer, apiPrefix)
876: 	// No peer-to-peer discovery for legacy API group.
877: 	wrapped := discoveryendpoint.WrapAggregatedDiscoveryToHandler(legacyRootAPIHandler, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager)
878: 	s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(wrapped.GenerateWebService("/api", metav1.APIVersions{}))
879: 	s.registerStorageReadinessCheck("", apiGroupInfo)
880: 
881: 	return nil
882: }
883: 
884: // InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.
885: // The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the
886: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.
887: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {
888: 	for _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {
889: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {
890: 			return fmt.Errorf("no version priority set for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
891: 		}
892: 		// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.
893: 		// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin
894: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {
895: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
896: 		}
897: 		if len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {
898: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v", *apiGroupInfo)
899: 		}
900: 	}
